subject: Anthropology
title: Out of Africa
description: A quick win: understand Out of Africa.

hook.text: Every human alive today descends from a small group that left Africa roughly 70,000 years ago. One exodus.

buildup.text: Genetic and fossil evidence supports the Out of Africa model: modern humans evolved in Africa and then spread globally.

discovery.text: They crossed into the Middle East, then reached Australia by 65,000 years ago and Europe by 45,000 years ago.

twist.text: Along the way, they met and interbred with Neanderthals and Denisovans — our migration was never a clean sweep.

climax.text: Out of Africa explains why all humans are one species. Our diversity is young; our shared ancestry is deep.

punchline.text: We all walked the same path out of Africa.

quiz.question: What does the Out of Africa model propose?
options: Humans evolved independently on each continent
Modern humans evolved in Africa and spread globally
Africa was the last continent settled
